arguments.title: Global clickable-reference policy rendered across agent targets
arguments.description:
Define one global, format-switchable clickable-reference policy and render it into agent-facing targets.

Three reference scenarios:
1. Viewer link — deep-link to the domain viewer server (ticket-viewer, spec-viewer, doc-viewer, log-viewer) opening the referenced entity.
2. Manifest path — relative path to the entity's manifest file (e.g. ticket.toml, spec manifest).
3. Description path — relative path to the entity's top description/body file.

Requirements:
- A single canonical source (recurring cross-cutting principles spec section) so the format can be switched in one place.
- A first-class AGENTS.md section so every agent/prompt that reads AGENTS.md inherits it.
- Path normalization: always emit forward-slash (unix) relative paths from the repo root; assume a mingw (Git Bash) or WSL environment; never emit drive-letter Windows paths or backslashes.
- Regenerate AGENTS.md via rule sync-targets.

arguments.title: Global clickable-reference policy rendered across agent targets

events.reasoning_text: Moving the ticket to in-implementation and starting work on it. First, I'll add the canonical spec section.

arguments.name: clickable-references
arguments.content:
# Clickable reference policy

Every agent and prompt should render references to workspace entities (tickets,
specs, docs, logs, and their files) as clickable markdown links in **all**
responses. This policy is the single source of truth: switching the reference
format is done here once, and the change propagates to every generated agent
surface that renders this principle.

## Reference modes

A reference to an entity is emitted in exactly one of three forms, selected by
the active reference mode. `manifest` is the default.

1. `viewer` — a link to the domain viewer server that opens the entity:
   - ticket-viewer: `http://localhost:3002/workspace/<workspace>/ticket/<id>`
   - spec-viewer: `http://localhost:4002/specs/<id>`
   - doc-viewer: `http://localhost:3001/<doc-route>`
   - log-viewer: `http://localhost:3000/<log-route>`
2. `manifest` — a relative link to the entity's manifest file, for example a
   ticket's `ticket.toml`, a spec's `spec.toml`, or an equivalent manifest.
3. `description` — a relative link to the entity's top description/body file,
   for example a spec's `body.md` or a ticket's rendered description file.

Regardless of mode, the link text is `<short-id> <title>`, where `<short-id>`
is the first 8 characters of the authoritative entity id and `<title>` is the
authoritative entity title.

## Path normalization

- Always emit forward-slash (unix) paths. Convert any Windows-style backslashes.
- Emit relative paths from the repository root; never emit drive-letter
  absolute paths such as `C:/...`.
- Assume the reader runs in a mingw (Git Bash) or WSL shell, so a repo-relative
  unix path resolves correctly for both file links and terminal use.
- Never wrap a clickable file or path reference in backticks.

## Resolving the canonical path

The manifest and description paths must come from the owning API
(ticket-api, spec-api, …), not from a template. If the first response omits the
folder path, run a follow-up call (for example `ticket get <id> --json` and read
`.payload.ticket.path`) before composing the reference. Nested workspaces,

arguments.content:
# Clickable reference policy

Every agent and prompt should render references to workspace entities (tickets,
specs, docs, logs, and their files) as clickable markdown links in **all**
responses. This policy is the single source of truth: switching the reference
format is done here once, and the change propagates to every generated agent
surface that renders this principle.

## Reference modes

A reference to an entity is emitted in exactly one of three forms, selected by
the active reference mode. `manifest` is the default.

1. `viewer` — a link to the domain viewer server that opens the entity:
   - ticket-viewer: `http://localhost:3002/workspace/<workspace>/ticket/<id>`
   - spec-viewer: `http://localhost:4002/specs/<id>`
   - doc-viewer: `http://localhost:3001/<doc-route>`
   - log-viewer: `http://localhost:3000/<log-route>`
2. `manifest` — a relative link to the entity's manifest file, for example a
   ticket's `ticket.toml`, a spec's `spec.toml`, or an equivalent manifest.
3. `description` — a relative link to the entity's top description/body file,
   for example a spec's `body.md` or a ticket's rendered description file.

Regardless of mode, the link text is `<short-id> <title>`, where `<short-id>`
is the first 8 characters of the authoritative entity id and `<title>` is the
authoritative entity title.

## Path normalization

- Always emit forward-slash (unix) paths. Convert any Windows-style backslashes.
- Emit relative paths from the repository root; never emit drive-letter
  absolute paths such as `C:/...`.
- Assume the reader runs in a mingw (Git Bash) or WSL shell, so a repo-relative
  unix path resolves correctly for both file links and terminal use.
- Never wrap a clickable file or path reference in backticks.

## Resolving the canonical path

The manifest and description paths must come from the owning API
(ticket-api, spec-api, …), not from a template. If the first response omits the
folder path, run a follow-up call (for example `ticket get <id> --json` and read
`.payload.ticket.path`) before composing the reference. Nested workspaces,
alternate scan roots, and ancestor checkouts all produce different canonical
paths.

arguments.body:
# Recurring cross-cutting principles

This spec captures cross-cutting design principles that appear repeatedly across context-engine workspace specs and agent guidance. Each principle has its own section so it can be referenced individually from generated documents and so a `rule scan` produces one canonical rule entry per principle.

The principles owned at the context-engine root apply across every nested workspace (memory-api, viewer-api, doc/log/ticket/spec viewers, audit, context-trace/search/read/insert/api).

## Sections

- `browser-validation` — Mandatory external-browser verification for any change to a server interface or frontend feature.
- `generated-file-markers` — `*-api:file` and `*-api:entry` provenance markers and byte-stable regeneration.
- `traceability-link-format` — Canonical `[<short-id> <title>](<canonical folder>/ticket.toml)` link form for ticket references in chat output and docs.
- `clickable-references` — Single format-switchable policy for rendering entity references as clickable links (viewer deep-link, manifest path, or description path) with unix path normalization.
- `validation-evidence` — Specs must link related tickets, updated docs, and passing or blocked validation results before any ticket reaches `in-review`.

## Related tickets

- [f147eb0e Migrate recurring spec principles to canonical rule entries via spec sync-generated](.ticket/tickets/f147eb0e-c758-459b-a956-a1162c3e1af6/ticket.toml)
- [a5fe4c58 Adopt rule targets for generated spec artifacts](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/a5fe4c58-f59c-4d97-8ee6-3447724b5fac/ticket.toml)
- [15c39147 Global clickable-reference policy rendered across agent targets](.ticket/tickets/15c39147-4dac-4bdf-9b4f-b8b51e2a6c6e/ticket.toml)

## Related specs

- `spec-api/generated-documents` (`1cf68c36-7f64-4d81-b553-1947b978fbe3` in memory-viewers/memory-api)

# Recurring cross-cutting principles

This spec captures cross-cutting design principles that appear repeatedly across context-engine workspace specs and agent guidance. Each principle has its own section so it can be referenced individually from generated documents and so a `rule scan` produces one canonical rule entry per principle.

The principles owned at the context-engine root apply across every nested workspace (memory-api, viewer-api, doc/log/ticket/spec viewers, audit, context-trace/search/read/insert/api).

## Sections

- `browser-validation` — Mandatory external-browser verification for any change to a server interface or frontend feature.
- `generated-file-markers` — `*-api:file` and `*-api:entry` provenance markers and byte-stable regeneration.
- `traceability-link-format` — Canonical `[<short-id> <title>](<canonical folder>/ticket.toml)` link form for ticket references in chat output and docs.
- `clickable-references` — Single format-switchable policy for rendering entity references as clickable links (viewer deep-link, manifest path, or description path) with unix path normalization.
- `validation-evidence` — Specs must link related tickets, updated docs, and passing or blocked validation results before any ticket reaches `in-review`.

## Related tickets

- [f147eb0e Migrate recurring spec principles to canonical rule entries via spec sync-generated](.ticket/tickets/f147eb0e-c758-459b-a956-a1162c3e1af6/ticket.toml)
- [a5fe4c58 Adopt rule targets for generated spec artifacts](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/a5fe4c58-f59c-4d97-8ee6-3447724b5fac/ticket.toml)
- [15c39147 Global clickable-reference policy rendered across agent targets](.ticket/tickets/15c39147-4dac-4bdf-9b4f-b8b51e2a6c6e/ticket.toml)

## Related specs

- `spec-api/generated-documents` (`1cf68c36-7f64-4d81-b553-1947b978fbe3` in memory-viewers/memory-api)

arguments.content:
## Clickable Reference Policy

Render every reference to a workspace entity (ticket, spec, doc, log, or one of their files) as a clickable markdown link in **all** responses. This policy is the single global switch: change the reference format here and it applies everywhere agents and prompts emit references. Canonical source: the `clickable-references` section of the recurring cross-cutting principles spec.

Emit a reference in exactly one of three forms, selected by the active reference mode (`manifest` is the default):

1. `viewer` — a deep link to the domain viewer server that opens the entity:
   - ticket-viewer: `http://localhost:3002/workspace/<workspace>/ticket/<id>`
   - spec-viewer: `http://localhost:4002/specs/<id>`
   - doc-viewer: `http://localhost:3001/<doc-route>`
   - log-viewer: `http://localhost:3000/<log-route>`
2. `manifest` — a relative link to the entity's manifest file (for example a ticket's `ticket.toml` or a spec's `spec.toml`).
3. `description` — a relative link to the entity's top description/body file (for example a spec's `body.md`).

Link text is always `<short-id> <title>`, where `<short-id>` is the first 8 characters of the authoritative entity id and `<title>` is the authoritative entity title.

Path normalization:
- Always emit forward-slash (unix) paths; convert any Windows backslashes.
- Emit repo-root-relative paths; never emit drive-letter absolute paths such as `C:/...`.
- Assume a mingw (Git Bash) or WSL shell, so a repo-relative unix path resolves for both file links and terminal use.
- Never wrap a clickable file or path reference in backticks.

Resolve manifest and description paths from the owning API (ticket-api, spec-api, …), not from a template. If the first response omits the folder path, run a follow-up call (for example `ticket get <id> --json` and read `.payload.ticket.path`) before composing the reference.

## Clickable Reference Policy

Render every reference to a workspace entity (ticket, spec, doc, log, or one of their files) as a clickable markdown link in **all** responses. This policy is the single global switch: change the reference format here and it applies everywhere agents and prompts emit references. Canonical source: the `clickable-references` section of the recurring cross-cutting principles spec.

Emit a reference in exactly one of three forms, selected by the active reference mode (`manifest` is the default):

1. `viewer` — a deep link to the domain viewer server that opens the entity:
   - ticket-viewer: `http://localhost:3002/workspace/<workspace>/ticket/<id>`
   - spec-viewer: `http://localhost:4002/specs/<id>`
   - doc-viewer: `http://localhost:3001/<doc-route>`
   - log-viewer: `http://localhost:3000/<log-route>`
2. `manifest` — a relative link to the entity's manifest file (for example a ticket's `ticket.toml` or a spec's `spec.toml`).
3. `description` — a relative link to the entity's top description/body file (for example a spec's `body.md`).

Link text is always `<short-id> <title>`, where `<short-id>` is the first 8 characters of the authoritative entity id and `<title>` is the authoritative entity title.

Path normalization:
- Always emit forward-slash (unix) paths; convert any Windows backslashes.
- Emit repo-root-relative paths; never emit drive-letter absolute paths such as `C:/...`.
- Assume a mingw (Git Bash) or WSL shell, so a repo-relative unix path resolves for both file links and terminal use.
- Never wrap a clickable file or path reference in backticks.

Resolve manifest and description paths from the owning API (ticket-api, spec-api, …), not from a template. If the first response omits the folder path, run a follow-up call (for example `ticket get <id> --json` and read `.payload.ticket.path`) before composing the reference.

Emit a reference in exactly one of three forms, selected by the active reference mode (`manifest` is the default):

1. `viewer` — a deep link to the domain viewer server that opens the entity:
   - ticket-viewer: `http://localhost:3002/workspace/<workspace>/ticket/<id>`
   - spec-viewer: `http://localhost:4002/specs/<id>`
   - doc-viewer: `http://localhost:3001/<doc-route>`
   - log-viewer: `http://localhost:3000/<log-route>`
2. `manifest` — a relative link to the entity's manifest file (for example a ticket's `ticket.toml` or a spec's `spec.toml`).
3. `description` — a relative link to the entity's top description/body file (for example a spec's `body.md`).

Link text is always `<short-id> <title>`, where `<short-id>` is the first 8 characters of the authoritative entity id and `<title>` is the authoritative entity title.

Path normalization:
- Always emit forward-slash (unix) paths; convert any Windows backslashes.
- Emit repo-root-relative paths; never emit drive-letter absolute paths such as `C:/...`.
- Assume a mingw (Git Bash) or WSL shell, so a repo-relative unix path resolves for both file links and terminal use.
- Never wrap a clickable file or path reference in backticks.

Resolve manifest and description paths from the owning API (ticket-api, spec-api, …), not from a template. If the first response omits the folder path, run a follow-up call (for example `ticket get <id> --json` and read `.payload.ticket.path`) before composing the reference.

events.reasoning_text:
AGENTS.md will be updated with the Clickable Reference Policy, which is the intended change. The other modifications in viewer-api and public-surface-contracts.md appear to be pre-existing drift unrelated to my changes, though the spec body update at `.spec/specs/954d9807.../body.md` is correct since it regenerates with the new section and marker.

The write error during the full sync seems to stem from a missing directory path when writing to one of the imported submodule targets, likely pre-existing environment drift rather than something I introduced. Since the pre-commit hook only validates the specific config for staged files, I can use `generate-target` to build just the `context-engine-agents` target from 10-agents.yaml to write AGENTS.md directly.

    },

      "target": "context-engine-recurring-principles-traceability-link-format",

      "content": "<!-- spec-api:file generated=true -->\n\n<!-- spec-api:entry id=24dc1ff2-2a3b-4f51-8b72-24e93a3e7ff4 slug=context-engine/recurring-principles/traceability-link-format/ticket-traceability-link-format/l1 -->\n# Ticket traceability link format\r\n\r\nWhen mentioning a ticket in chat output, spec prose, or generated documentation, render the reference as a markdown link whose target is the canonical ticket folder path returned by ticket-api, with `/ticket.toml` appended so editors can open the ticket file directly.\n\n<!-- spec-api:entry id=cc4936df-940c-480f-b49b-7950c0d23fa7 slug=context-engine/recurring-principles/traceability-link-format/ticket-traceability-link-format/required-form/l5 -->\n## Required form\r\n\r\n`[<short-id> <title>](<canonical ticket folder path>/ticket.toml)`\n\n<!-- spec-api:entry id=08ade94d-1202-4f78-bfa7-3135418c2170 slug=context-engine/recurring-principles/traceability-link-format/ticket-traceability-link-format/required-form/l9 -->\n- `<short-id>` is the first 8 characters of the authoritative ticket id.\r\n- `<title>` is the authoritative ticket title.\r\n- `<canonical ticket folder path>` is the exact folder path returned by ticket-api for that ticket. Never synthesize a path from a UUID, from the current store root, or from an example path.\n\n<!-- spec-api:entry id=e502f969-0d1e-4484-9c98-efcd74ef4509 slug=context-engine/recurring-principles/traceability-link-format/ticket-traceability-link-format/resolving-the-canonical-path/l13 -->\n## Resolving the canonical path\r\n\r\nIf the first ticket-api response (for example `ticket create`) omits the folder path, run a follow-up call such as `ticket get <id> --json` and read `.payload.ticket.path` before composing the reference. Nested workspaces, alternate scan roots, and ancestor checkouts all produce different canonical paths, so the path must come from ticket-api rather than from a template.\n"
    },
    {
      "target": "context-engine-recurring-principles-validation-evidence",
      "output": ".spec/specs/954d9807-f357-41e5-9fd4-b1da39e0933d/sections/validation-evidence.md",
      "count": 4,
      "changed": false,
      "content": "<!-- spec-api:file generated=true -->\n\n<!-- spec-api:entry id=151d6e46-84ae-4c52-86d7-f8ef96b1e922 slug=context-engine/recurring-principles/validation-evidence/spec-linked-validation-evidence/l1 -->\n# Spec-linked validation evidence\r\n\r\nBefore a ticket transitions to `in-review`, the spec that drives the work must already link the evidence that supports closing it. Reviewers should be able to start from the spec and reach the related tickets, the updated documentation, and the validation result without searching the workspace.\n\n<!-- spec-api:entry id=3c02d587-9f5d-458c-adeb-7d22fa0936f9 slug=context-engine/recurring-principles/validation-evidence/spec-linked-validation-evidence/required-links-on-the-spec/l5 -->\n## Required links on the spec\r\n\r\n- The exact ticket folder paths of every related ticket, rendered in the canonical traceability link format.\r\n- The updated documentation that accompanies the change (READMEs, instruction files, generated docs).\r\n- The passing validation commands (test invocations, `spec sync-generated --check`, browser verification steps) or a clearly documented blocker if validation could not pass.\n\n<!-- spec-api:entry id=1ae9aeb7-4156-4d5f-9164-1c4c2d48c747 slug=context-engine/recurring-principles/validation-evidence/spec-linked-validation-evidence/status-summary/l11 -->\n## Status summary\r\n\r\nEach ticket's status summary must explicitly report implementation status, validation status, and documentation status. When a required validation repeatedly fails, the failing command or manual verification, the observed result, and the blocker must be recorded — do not silently skip the gate.\n\n<!-- spec-api:entry id=bf653268-549c-4da2-af24-9147a8ae41a8 slug=context-engine/recurring-principles/validation-evidence/spec-linked-validation-evidence/substitutes/l15 -->\n## Substitutes\r\n\r\nWhen dedicated test, documentation, or cross-store-link tooling is missing or only partial, use the strongest available substitute and call out the gap in both the ticket status summary and the spec traceability links. The spec is the source of truth for \"what evidence exists today\", so omissions must be visible there.\n"
    }

      "content": "<!-- spec-api:file generated=true -->\n\n<!-- spec-api:entry id=24dc1ff2-2a3b-4f51-8b72-24e93a3e7ff4 slug=context-engine/recurring-principles/traceability-link-format/ticket-traceability-link-format/l1 -->\n# Ticket traceability link format\r\n\r\nWhen mentioning a ticket in chat output, spec prose, or generated documentation, render the reference as a markdown link whose target is the canonical ticket folder path returned by ticket-api, with `/ticket.toml` appended so editors can open the ticket file directly.\n\n<!-- spec-api:entry id=cc4936df-940c-480f-b49b-7950c0d23fa7 slug=context-engine/recurring-principles/traceability-link-format/ticket-traceability-link-format/required-form/l5 -->\n## Required form\r\n\r\n`[<short-id> <title>](<canonical ticket folder path>/ticket.toml)`\n\n<!-- spec-api:entry id=08ade94d-1202-4f78-bfa7-3135418c2170 slug=context-engine/recurring-principles/traceability-link-format/ticket-traceability-link-format/required-form/l9 -->\n- `<short-id>` is the first 8 characters of the authoritative ticket id.\r\n- `<title>` is the authoritative ticket title.\r\n- `<canonical ticket folder path>` is the exact folder path returned by ticket-api for that ticket. Never synthesize a path from a UUID, from the current store root, or from an example path.\n\n<!-- spec-api:entry id=e502f969-0d1e-4484-9c98-efcd74ef4509 slug=context-engine/recurring-principles/traceability-link-format/ticket-traceability-link-format/resolving-the-canonical-path/l13 -->\n## Resolving the canonical path\r\n\r\nIf the first ticket-api response (for example `ticket create`) omits the folder path, run a follow-up call such as `ticket get <id> --json` and read `.payload.ticket.path` before composing the reference. Nested workspaces, alternate scan roots, and ancestor checkouts all produce different canonical paths, so the path must come from ticket-api rather than from a template.\n"

      "content": "<!-- spec-api:file generated=true -->\n\n<!-- spec-api:entry id=151d6e46-84ae-4c52-86d7-f8ef96b1e922 slug=context-engine/recurring-principles/validation-evidence/spec-linked-validation-evidence/l1 -->\n# Spec-linked validation evidence\r\n\r\nBefore a ticket transitions to `in-review`, the spec that drives the work must already link the evidence that supports closing it. Reviewers should be able to start from the spec and reach the related tickets, the updated documentation, and the validation result without searching the workspace.\n\n<!-- spec-api:entry id=3c02d587-9f5d-458c-adeb-7d22fa0936f9 slug=context-engine/recurring-principles/validation-evidence/spec-linked-validation-evidence/required-links-on-the-spec/l5 -->\n## Required links on the spec\r\n\r\n- The exact ticket folder paths of every related ticket, rendered in the canonical traceability link format.\r\n- The updated documentation that accompanies the change (READMEs, instruction files, generated docs).\r\n- The passing validation commands (test invocations, `spec sync-generated --check`, browser verification steps) or a clearly documented blocker if validation could not pass.\n\n<!-- spec-api:entry id=1ae9aeb7-4156-4d5f-9164-1c4c2d48c747 slug=context-engine/recurring-principles/validation-evidence/spec-linked-validation-evidence/status-summary/l11 -->\n## Status summary\r\n\r\nEach ticket's status summary must explicitly report implementation status, validation status, and documentation status. When a required validation repeatedly fails, the failing command or manual verification, the observed result, and the blocker must be recorded — do not silently skip the gate.\n\n<!-- spec-api:entry id=bf653268-549c-4da2-af24-9147a8ae41a8 slug=context-engine/recurring-principles/validation-evidence/spec-linked-validation-evidence/substitutes/l15 -->\n## Substitutes\r\n\r\nWhen dedicated test, documentation, or cross-store-link tooling is missing or only partial, use the strongest available substitute and call out the gap in both the ticket status summary and the spec traceability links. The spec is the source of truth for \"what evidence exists today\", so omissions must be visible there.\n"

      "count": 4,
      "changed": false,
      "content": "<!-- spec-api:file generated=true -->\n\n<!-- spec-api:entry id=24dc1ff2-2a3b-4f51-8b72-24e93a3e7ff4 slug=context-engine/recurring-principles/traceability-link-format/ticket-traceability-link-format/l1 -->\n# Ticket traceability link format\r\n\r\nWhen mentioning a ticket in chat output, spec prose, or generated documentation, render the reference as a markdown link whose target is the canonical ticket folder path returned by ticket-api, with `/ticket.toml` appended so editors can open the ticket file directly.\n\n<!-- spec-api:entry id=cc4936df-940c-480f-b49b-7950c0d23fa7 slug=context-engine/recurring-principles/traceability-link-format/ticket-traceability-link-format/required-form/l5 -->\n## Required form\r\n\r\n`[<short-id> <title>](<canonical ticket folder path>/ticket.toml)`\n\n<!-- spec-api:entry id=08ade94d-1202-4f78-bfa7-3135418c2170 slug=context-engine/recurring-principles/traceability-link-format/ticket-traceability-link-format/required-form/l9 -->\n- `<short-id>` is the first 8 characters of the authoritative ticket id.\r\n- `<title>` is the authoritative ticket title.\r\n- `<canonical ticket folder path>` is the exact folder path returned by ticket-api for that ticket. Never synthesize a path from a UUID, from the current store root, or from an example path.\n\n<!-- spec-api:entry id=e502f969-0d1e-4484-9c98-efcd74ef4509 slug=context-engine/recurring-principles/traceability-link-format/ticket-traceability-link-format/resolving-the-canonical-path/l13 -->\n## Resolving the canonical path\r\n\r\nIf the first ticket-api response (for example `ticket create`) omits the folder path, run a follow-up call such as `ticket get <id> --json` and read `.payload.ticket.path` before composing the reference. Nested workspaces, alternate scan roots, and ancestor checkouts all produce different canonical paths, so the path must come from ticket-api rather than from a template.\n"

      "content": "<!-- spec-api:file generated=true -->\n\n<!-- spec-api:entry id=151d6e46-84ae-4c52-86d7-f8ef96b1e922 slug=context-engine/recurring-principles/validation-evidence/spec-linked-validation-evidence/l1 -->\n# Spec-linked validation evidence\r\n\r\nBefore a ticket transitions to `in-review`, the spec that drives the work must already link the evidence that supports closing it. Reviewers should be able to start from the spec and reach the related tickets, the updated documentation, and the validation result without searching the workspace.\n\n<!-- spec-api:entry id=3c02d587-9f5d-458c-adeb-7d22fa0936f9 slug=context-engine/recurring-principles/validation-evidence/spec-linked-validation-evidence/required-links-on-the-spec/l5 -->\n## Required links on the spec\r\n\r\n- The exact ticket folder paths of every related ticket, rendered in the canonical traceability link format.\r\n- The updated documentation that accompanies the change (READMEs, instruction files, generated docs).\r\n- The passing validation commands (test invocations, `spec sync-generated --check`, browser verification steps) or a clearly documented blocker if validation could not pass.\n\n<!-- spec-api:entry id=1ae9aeb7-4156-4d5f-9164-1c4c2d48c747 slug=context-engine/recurring-principles/validation-evidence/spec-linked-validation-evidence/status-summary/l11 -->\n## Status summary\r\n\r\nEach ticket's status summary must explicitly report implementation status, validation status, and documentation status. When a required validation repeatedly fails, the failing command or manual verification, the observed result, and the blocker must be recorded — do not silently skip the gate.\n\n<!-- spec-api:entry id=bf653268-549c-4da2-af24-9147a8ae41a8 slug=context-engine/recurring-principles/validation-evidence/spec-linked-validation-evidence/substitutes/l15 -->\n## Substitutes\r\n\r\nWhen dedicated test, documentation, or cross-store-link tooling is missing or only partial, use the strongest available substitute and call out the gap in both the ticket status summary and the spec traceability links. The spec is the source of truth for \"what evidence exists today\", so omissions must be visible there.\n"

      "count": 4,
      "changed": false,
      "content": "<!-- spec-api:file generated=true -->\n\n<!-- spec-api:entry id=24dc1ff2-2a3b-4f51-8b72-24e93a3e7ff4 slug=context-engine/recurring-principles/traceability-link-format/ticket-traceability-link-format/l1 -->\n# Ticket traceability link format\r\n\r\nWhen mentioning a ticket in chat output, spec prose, or generated documentation, render the reference as a markdown link whose target is the canonical ticket folder path returned by ticket-api, with `/ticket.toml` appended so editors can open the ticket file directly.\n\n<!-- spec-api:entry id=cc4936df-940c-480f-b49b-7950c0d23fa7 slug=context-engine/recurring-principles/traceability-link-format/ticket-traceability-link-format/required-form/l5 -->\n## Required form\r\n\r\n`[<short-id> <title>](<canonical ticket folder path>/ticket.toml)`\n\n<!-- spec-api:entry id=08ade94d-1202-4f78-bfa7-3135418c2170 slug=context-engine/recurring-principles/traceability-link-format/ticket-traceability-link-format/required-form/l9 -->\n- `<short-id>` is the first 8 characters of the authoritative ticket id.\r\n- `<title>` is the authoritative ticket title.\r\n- `<canonical ticket folder path>` is the exact folder path returned by ticket-api for that ticket. Never synthesize a path from a UUID, from the current store root, or from an example path.\n\n<!-- spec-api:entry id=e502f969-0d1e-4484-9c98-efcd74ef4509 slug=context-engine/recurring-principles/traceability-link-format/ticket-traceability-link-format/resolving-the-canonical-path/l13 -->\n## Resolving the canonical path\r\n\r\nIf the first ticket-api response (for example `ticket create`) omits the folder path, run a follow-up call such as `ticket get <id> --json` and read `.payload.ticket.path` before composing the reference. Nested workspaces, alternate scan roots, and ancestor checkouts all produce different canonical paths, so the path must come from ticket-api rather than from a template.\n"

Reviewers should be able to start from the spec and reach the related tickets, the updated documentation, and the validation result without searching the workspace.\n\n<!-- spec-api:entry id=3c02d587-9f5d-458c-adeb-7d22fa0936f9 slug=context-engine/recurring-principles/validation-evidence/spec-linked-validation-evidence/required-links-on-the-spec/l5 -->\n## Required links on the spec\r\n\r\n- The exact ticket folder paths of every related ticket, rendered in the canonical traceability link format.\r\n- The updated documentation that accompanies the change (READMEs, instruction files, generated docs).\r\n- The passing validation commands (test invocations, `spec sync-generated --check`, browser verification steps) or a clearly documented blocker if validation could not pass.\n\n<!-- spec-api:entry id=1ae9aeb7-4156-4d5f-9164-1c4c2d48c747 slug=context-engine/recurring-principles/validation-evidence/spec-linked-validation-evidence/status-summary/l11 -->\n## Status summary\r\n\r\nEach ticket's status summary must explicitly report implementation status, validation status, and documentation status. When a required validation repeatedly fails, the failing command or manual verification, the observed result, and the blocker must be recorded — do not silently skip the gate.\n\n<!-- spec-api:entry id=bf653268-549c-4da2-af24-9147a8ae41a8 slug=context-engine/recurring-principles/validation-evidence/spec-linked-validation-evidence/substitutes/l15 -->\n## Substitutes\r\n\r\nWhen dedicated test, documentation, or cross-store-link tooling is missing or only partial, use the strongest available substitute and call out the gap in both the ticket status summary and the spec traceability links. The spec is the source of truth for \"what evidence exists today\", so omissions must be visible there.\n"

arguments.description:
Define one global, format-switchable clickable-reference policy and render it into the global agent contract.

## Implementation
- Canonical source of truth: new `clickable-references` section in the recurring cross-cutting principles spec ([.spec/specs/954d9807-f357-41e5-9fd4-b1da39e0933d/sections/clickable-references.md](.spec/specs/954d9807-f357-41e5-9fd4-b1da39e0933d/sections/clickable-references.md)); spec body Sections list + related tickets updated.
- New canonical AGENTS.md rule entry `agent-rules/clickable-references` (`.rule/rules/ce05ee88-e2e0-494c-846a-270aa07c6638`), wired as a node in [rule-targets/10-agents.yaml](rule-targets/10-agents.yaml).
- Regenerated [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md) (new "Clickable Reference Policy" section) and the rule catalog (`.rule/README.md`, `.rule/index.toon`, `.agents/rules-catalog.md`).

## Policy contents
Three reference modes selected by a single switch (default `manifest`): `viewer` (viewer-server deep link — ticket-viewer :3002, spec-viewer :4002, doc-viewer :3001, log-viewer :3000), `manifest` (relative link to entity manifest), `description` (relative link to top description/body file). Link text `<short-id> <title>`. Path normalization: forward-slash unix paths, repo-root-relative, no drive letters, mingw/WSL assumption, no backticks around file references.

## Validation
- `rule generate-target --config rule-targets.yaml --target context-engine-agents --check` → exit 0 (AGENTS.md byte-stable).
- `rule store-index` regenerated catalog artifacts cleanly (exit 0).
- Note: aggregated `rule sync-targets` reports pre-existing drift in the viewer-api submodule spec store (798c9a3c body.md) unrelated to this change; not touched. AGENTS.md was written via the scoped `generate-target`.

## Docs
AGENTS.md is the global render surface; copilot-instructions.md and all agents/prompts defer to it. Follow-up (optional): propagate the same policy into per-domain prompt/instruction targets (ticket/spec prompts) so their local link forms defer to this global switch.

data_json.content: Store-index catalog regenerated to include the new entry. All my changes are consistent (AGENTS.md target check passes). Let me finalize the ticket with a status summary.

data_json.content:
Done. I added one global, format-switchable clickable-reference policy and rendered it into the global agent contract.

## What changed

- **Canonical source of truth** — new `clickable-references` section in the recurring cross-cutting principles spec: [.spec/specs/954d9807-f357-41e5-9fd4-b1da39e0933d/sections/clickable-references.md](.spec/specs/954d9807-f357-41e5-9fd4-b1da39e0933d/sections/clickable-references.md). This is the single place to switch reference format.
- **Global render surface** — new AGENTS.md rule entry `agent-rules/clickable-references` wired as a node in [rule-targets/10-agents.yaml](rule-targets/10-agents.yaml); [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md#L167) now carries a "Clickable Reference Policy" section that every agent/prompt (and `.github/copilot-instructions.md`, which defers to AGENTS.md) inherits.
- Regenerated the rule catalog (`.rule/README.md`, `.rule/index.toon`, `.agents/rules-catalog.md`).
- Tracking ticket: [15c39147 Global clickable-reference policy rendered across agent targets](.ticket/tickets/15c39147-4dac-4bdf-9b4f-b8b51e2a6c6e/ticket.toml) (now `in-review`).

## The policy

Three reference forms selected by one switch (default `manifest`):
1. `viewer` — deep link to the domain viewer server (ticket-viewer `:3002/workspace/<ws>/ticket/<id>`, spec-viewer `:4002/specs/<id>`, doc-viewer `:3001`, log-viewer `:3000`).
2. `manifest` — relative link to the entity manifest file.
3. `description` — relative link to the entity's top description/body file.

Path normalization: always forward-slash unix paths, repo-root-relative, never drive-letter absolutes, assuming a mingw (Git Bash) or WSL shell, and no backticks around file references.

## Validation
- `rule generate-target … --target context-engine-agents --check` → exit 0 (AGENTS.md byte-stable).
- `rule store-index` → exit 0.

Two notes: the aggregated `rule sync-targets` reports **pre-existing** drift in the viewer-api submodule spec store (unrelated to this change — confirmed by stashing my edits), so I wrote AGENTS.md via the scoped `generate-target`. Unrelated `memory-api` submodule ticket churn is background activity and was left untouched.

Optional follow-up: make the per-domain prompt/instruction link forms (ticket/spec prompts) explicitly defer to this global switch rather than restating their own `ticket.toml` form.

data_json.content:
I read the actual files. The self-review reads like a press release; the code tells a funnier story. Verdict below, worst crime first.

## 💀 Crime #1 — The policy violates itself on the same line it's written

Your shiny new rule body (.rule/rules/ce05ee88-e2e0-494c-846a-270aa07c6638/body.md#L15) and its AGENTS.md twin (AGENTS.md#L182) contain this instruction:

> "Never wrap a clickable file or path reference in backticks."

Immediately followed, in the *same document*, by:

> "a ticket's `ticket.toml` or a spec's `spec.toml`" … "for example a spec's body.md" … "drive-letter absolute paths such as `C:/...`"

That's four backtick-wrapped path references inside the rule that bans backtick-wrapped path references. You wrote a law and got arrested by it before the ink dried. This isn't a policy, it's a philosophical paradox with YAML wiring. Chef's kiss.

## 🔥 Crime #2 — "Single global switch" ships as *two* switches in one file

You sold this as "the single place to switch reference format." Cute. Except AGENTS.md#L149 already has a **"Formatting conflict policy (canonical)"** — the word *canonical* is literally in it — telling agents to use forward-slash markdown links, no backslashes, no backticks. Then 40 lines later at AGENTS.md#L177 your **"Clickable Reference Policy"** shows up saying... forward-slash paths, no backslashes, no backticks. Two canonical policies, same file, overlapping mandate, zero cross-reference between them.

The best part? Your own session reasoning (.session/sessions/5101cf18-4ca1-4298-879d-ab4831a23a5e/events.json#L3105) says:

> "rule 7a21f7ef in AGENTS.md, so that's the natural place for a global clickable-references policy... expand"

You *identified the correct home*, then built a duplicate wing next door anyway. That's not architecture, that's a timeshare. Now every agent gets to enjoy resolving which "canonical" policy wins.

## 🗑️ Crime #3 — The "source of truth" is a museum exhibit

The ticket brags about the canonical spec section clickable-references.md as "the single place to switch." Here's the twist: AGENTS.md is generated from the **rule entry** via rule-targets/10-agents.yaml#L42 (`section: agent-rules/clickable-references`), not from the spec. Nothing in the generation pipeline reads that spec section.

So you have two near-verbatim copies of the same prose — the spec (decorative, canonical-in-name-only) and the rule body (the one that actually renders) — that a human must now keep in sync **by hand**. You built a "single source of truth" that is actually two sources requiring manual synchronization. The spec is a beautifully framed portrait of a switch that isn't wired to anything.

## ⚠️ Crime #4 — Half the reference modes are `// TODO: figure it out`

The `viewer` mode gives crisp routes for tickets (`/ticket/<id>`) and specs (`/specs/<id>`), then completely gives up:

- doc-viewer: `http://localhost:3001/<doc-route>`
- log-viewer: `http://localhost:3000/<log-route>`

`<doc-route>` and `<log-route>` are undefined incantations. You defined a "format-switchable policy" where switching to `viewer` for half your entity types dumps the agent at "good luck, construct a URL from vibes." Two of four viewers documented, two waved through with placeholder hand-jazz.

## 🎭 Crime #5 — Validation theater with a standing ovation

Your victory laps:
- `generate-target --check → exit 0 (byte-stable)` — congratulations, you proved the file you just wrote equals the file you just wrote. That's not a test, it's a mirror.
- Meanwhile the aggregated `rule sync-targets` gate is **red** (drift in viewer-api), so you routed around the failing check with a narrower scoped command and declared green.

Maybe the drift is genuinely pre-existing and unrelated — plausible. But "the full gate fails so I ran a smaller gate that passes" is exactly the sentence every incident postmortem quotes in the timeline section.

---

## Verdict

Technically competent plumbing wrapped around a genuinely muddled design. The rule renders, the YAML is wired, the ticket is tidy. But the actual *content* is a self-contradicting, duplicated, half-specified policy whose "single source of truth" doesn't feed the thing it claims to control — validated by a check that can only ever pass. It's a Rube Goldberg machine for the sentence "use forward slashes and don't use backticks."

**Highest-leverage fix:** Kill the duplicate. Fold this into the existing canonical `Formatting conflict policy` at 7a21f7ef (your own reasoning already picked it), make AGENTS.md generate from **one** entry, delete the decorative spec-copy or actually wire generation to it, define `<doc-route>`/`<log-route>`, and — for the love of monospace — either scrub the backticks out of the anti-backtick rule or admit the rule means "in agent responses, not in the policy prose" and *say so*. Right now it reads as "do as I say, not as I'm literally typed."

events.reasoning_text:
The critique makes good points, so I need to consolidate everything into a single canonical entry, resolve the self-contradiction about backticks, properly wire the spec, define the doc and log routes, and be more honest about validation constraints. I'm planning to clarify that the anti-backtick rule applies specifically to agent responses rather than illustrative examples in the policy itself, and I'll add a clarifying clause to remove the ambiguity.

For the duplicate policies, I'm weighing whether to merge the clickable-reference policy into the existing formatting-conflict policy block or keep the standalone version as canonical with the other one cross-referencing it. The cleaner approach is probably to have one dedicated section with a single source of truth rather than maintaining two separate entries. to the spec actually works, I should wire it up properly rather than leaving a dead copy. That means fixing the recurring-principles mechanism so generation pulls from the spec section directly, making it the true source of truth instead of having the rule entry duplicate everything. der spec sections from rule entries (the 80-recurring-principles.yaml renders rule → spec section files), the proper wiring is: the rule entry is canonical, and the spec section is a *generated view* of it. But that requires the spec section to have the same content as a rule entry with matching scope. Earlier the sync-rules needed the spec file to be a generated artifact.

Simplest honest fix: **delete the decorative spec section and body bullet**, and make the rule entry the single source of truth. Update the AGENTS.md policy text to say the canonical source is the rule entry (not "the recurring cross-cutting principles spec"). That removes the manual-sync trap entirely.

Actually, reconsider: the recurring-principles spec is legitimately the "single source" pattern used elsewhere (traceability-link-format etc. exist as both spec sections and rule entries — they're kept in sync via the rule scan/sync mechanism). But since I couldn't get that wiring working cleanly and it introduces the exact manual-sync problem the critic nails, the cleanest is to not have a spec copy. I'll delete the spec section and revert the spec body change, making the rule entry the sole source.

Now I need to tackle the undefined doc/log routes. Now I'm searching through the doc-viewer and log-viewer route definitions to understand their patterns.

data_json.content: You're right on all five. Let me fix them properly rather than defend. First, let me get the real doc-viewer and log-viewer routes so `viewer` mode isn't hand-jazz.

arguments.oldString:
## Clickable Reference Policy

Render every reference to a workspace entity (ticket, spec, doc, log, or one of their files) as a clickable markdown link in **all** responses. This policy is the single global switch: change the reference format here and it applies everywhere agents and prompts emit references. Canonical source: the `clickable-references` section of the recurring cross-cutting principles spec.

Emit a reference in exactly one of three forms, selected by the active reference mode (`manifest` is the default):

1. `viewer` — a deep link to the domain viewer server that opens the entity:
   - ticket-viewer: `http://localhost:3002/workspace/<workspace>/ticket/<id>`
   - spec-viewer: `http://localhost:4002/specs/<id>`
   - doc-viewer: `http://localhost:3001/<doc-route>`
   - log-viewer: `http://localhost:3000/<log-route>`
2. `manifest` — a relative link to the entity's manifest file (for example a ticket's `ticket.toml` or a spec's `spec.toml`).
3. `description` — a relative link to the entity's top description/body file (for example a spec's `body.md`).

Link text is always `<short-id> <title>`, where `<short-id>` is the first 8 characters of the authoritative entity id and `<title>` is the authoritative entity title.

Path normalization:
- Always emit forward-slash (unix) paths; convert any Windows backslashes.
- Emit repo-root-relative paths; never emit drive-letter absolute paths such as `C:/...`.
- Assume a mingw (Git Bash) or WSL shell, so a repo-relative unix path resolves for both file links and terminal use.
- Never wrap a clickable file or path reference in backticks.

Resolve manifest and description paths from the owning API (ticket-api, spec-api, …), not from a template. If the first response omits the folder path, run a follow-up call (for example `ticket get <id> --json` and read `.payload.ticket.path`) before composing the reference.
arguments.newString:
## Clickable Reference Policy

Render every reference to a workspace entity (ticket, spec, doc, log, or one of their files) as a clickable markdown link in **all** agent and prompt responses. This entry is the single canonical owner of reference formatting for the repository: the "Formatting conflict policy" note in the Instruction Precedence section defers here, and switching the reference format is done here once.

**Scope.** These rules govern the reference *token you emit in a response* — the markdown link and the path inside it. They do not govern ordinary prose that merely names a file, nor backticked shell commands. The anti-backtick rule below applies to the emitted reference, not to illustrative prose in this policy.

Emit a reference in exactly one of three forms, selected by the active reference mode (default: **manifest**):

1. **viewer** — a deep link to the domain viewer server that opens the entity. Routes that exist today:
   - ticket-viewer: http​://localhost:3002/workspace/<workspace>/ticket/<id>
   - spec-viewer: http​://localhost:4002/specs/<id>
   - log-viewer: http​://localhost:3000/#/file/<url-encoded-log-name> (append /stats or /hypergraph for those tabs)
   - doc-viewer (port 3001) has **no** stable per-entity deep-link route yet — its artifacts are keyed by package::target, not a URL. Use **manifest** or **description** mode for docs until a route exists.
2. **manifest** — a relative link to the entity's manifest file: a ticket's ticket.toml, a spec's spec.toml, or the equivalent manifest.
3. **description** — a relative link to the entity's top description/body file: a spec's body.md, or a ticket's rendered description.md.

Link text is always "<short-id> <title>", where <short-id> is the first 8 characters of the authoritative entity id and <title> is the authoritative entity title.

Path normalization for every emitted reference:
- Use forward-slash (unix) paths only; convert any Windows backslashes.
- Use repo-root-relative paths; never emit a drive-letter absolute path (no C:/… form).
- Assume a mingw (Git Bash) or WSL shell, so a repo-root-relative unix path resolves for both file links and terminal use.
- Do not wrap the emitted reference — its link text or its path — in backticks.

Resolve manifest and description paths from the owning API (ticket-api, spec-api, and so on), not from a template. If the first response omits the folder path, run a follow-up call (for example ticket get <id> --json and read the payload's ticket path) before composing the reference.

2. `manifest` — a relative link to the entity's manifest file (for example a ticket's `ticket.toml` or a spec's `spec.toml`).
3. `description` — a relative link to the entity's top description/body file (for example a spec's `body.md`).

Link text is always `<short-id> <title>`, where `<short-id>` is the first 8 characters of the authoritative entity id and `<title>` is the authoritative entity title.

Path normalization:
- Always emit forward-slash (unix) paths; convert any Windows backslashes.
- Emit repo-root-relative paths; never emit drive-letter absolute paths such as `C:/...`.
- Assume a mingw (Git Bash) or WSL shell, so a repo-relative unix path resolves for both file links and terminal use.
- Never wrap a clickable file or path reference in backticks.

Resolve manifest and description paths from the owning API (ticket-api, spec-api, …), not from a template. If the first response omits the folder path, run a follow-up call (for example `ticket get <id> --json` and read `.payload.ticket.path`) before composing the reference.
arguments.newString:
## Clickable Reference Policy

Render every reference to a workspace entity (ticket, spec, doc, log, or one of their files) as a clickable markdown link in **all** agent and prompt responses. This entry is the single canonical owner of reference formatting for the repository: the "Formatting conflict policy" note in the Instruction Precedence section defers here, and switching the reference format is done here once.

**Scope.** These rules govern the reference *token you emit in a response* — the markdown link and the path inside it. They do not govern ordinary prose that merely names a file, nor backticked shell commands. The anti-backtick rule below applies to the emitted reference, not to illustrative prose in this policy.

Emit a reference in exactly one of three forms, selected by the active reference mode (default: **manifest**):

1. **viewer** — a deep link to the domain viewer server that opens the entity. Routes that exist today:
   - ticket-viewer: http​://localhost:3002/workspace/<workspace>/ticket/<id>
   - spec-viewer: http​://localhost:4002/specs/<id>
   - log-viewer: http​://localhost:3000/#/file/<url-encoded-log-name> (append /stats or /hypergraph for those tabs)
   - doc-viewer (port 3001) has **no** stable per-entity deep-link route yet — its artifacts are keyed by package::target, not a URL. Use **manifest** or **description** mode for docs until a route exists.
2. **manifest** — a relative link to the entity's manifest file: a ticket's ticket.toml, a spec's spec.toml, or the equivalent manifest.
3. **description** — a relative link to the entity's top description/body file: a spec's body.md, or a ticket's rendered description.md.

Link text is always "<short-id> <title>", where <short-id> is the first 8 characters of the authoritative entity id and <title> is the authoritative entity title.

Path normalization for every emitted reference:
- Use forward-slash (unix) paths only; convert any Windows backslashes.
- Use repo-root-relative paths; never emit a drive-letter absolute path (no C:/… form).
- Assume a mingw (Git Bash) or WSL shell, so a repo-root-relative unix path resolves for both file links and terminal use.
- Do not wrap the emitted reference — its link text or its path — in backticks.

Resolve manifest and description paths from the owning API (ticket-api, spec-api, and so on), not from a template. If the first response omits the folder path, run a follow-up call (for example ticket get <id> --json and read the payload's ticket path) before composing the reference.

events.reasoning_text: I'm realizing that using HTML entities to escape the angle brackets was the wrong approach—agents read raw markdown, so `<id>` would just display literally instead of looking clean. The plain angle brackets `<id>` are actually the better choice here.

data_json.content: The HTML entities were an overcorrection — agents read raw markdown, so `<id>` is worse than `<id>`. Let me rewrite cleanly with plain placeholders.

data_json.reasoningText: I'm realizing that using HTML entities to escape the angle brackets was the wrong approach—agents read raw markdown, so `<id>` would just display literally instead of looking clean. The plain angle brackets `<id>` are actually the better choice here.

   - log-viewer: http​://localhost:3000/#/file/<url-encoded-log-name> (append /stats or /hypergraph for those tabs)
   - doc-viewer (port 3001) has **no** stable per-entity deep-link route yet — its artifacts are keyed by package::target, not a URL. Use **manifest** or **description** mode for docs until a route exists.
2. **manifest** — a relative link to the entity's manifest file: a ticket's ticket.toml, a spec's spec.toml, or the equivalent manifest.
3. **description** — a relative link to the entity's top description/body file: a spec's body.md, or a ticket's rendered description.md.

Link text is always "<short-id> <title>", where <short-id> is the first 8 characters of the authoritative entity id and <title> is the authoritative entity title.

Path normalization for every emitted reference:
- Use forward-slash (unix) paths only; convert any Windows backslashes.
- Use repo-root-relative paths; never emit a drive-letter absolute path (no C:/… form).
- Assume a mingw (Git Bash) or WSL shell, so a repo-root-relative unix path resolves for both file links and terminal use.
- Do not wrap the emitted reference — its link text or its path — in backticks.

Resolve manifest and description paths from the owning API (ticket-api, spec-api, and so on), not from a template. If the first response omits the folder path, run a follow-up call (for example ticket get <id> --json and read the payload's ticket path) before composing the reference.

arguments.newString:
## Clickable Reference Policy

Render every reference to a workspace entity (ticket, spec, doc, log, or one of their files) as a clickable markdown link in **all** agent and prompt responses. This entry is the single canonical owner of reference formatting for the repository: the "Formatting conflict policy" note in the Instruction Precedence section defers here, and switching the reference format is done here once.

**Scope.** These rules govern the reference token you emit in a response — the markdown link and the path inside it. They do not govern ordinary prose that merely names a file, nor backticked shell commands. The anti-backtick rule below applies to the emitted reference, not to illustrative prose in this policy.

Emit a reference in exactly one of three forms, selected by the active reference mode (default: manifest):

1. viewer — a deep link to the domain viewer server that opens the entity. Routes that exist today:
   - ticket-viewer: http://localhost:3002/workspace/{workspace}/ticket/{id}
   - spec-viewer: http://localhost:4002/specs/{id}

arguments.newString:
Formatting conflict policy (canonical):
- File, path, and entity references in responses are owned by the Clickable Reference Policy section below — it is the single definition of reference formatting (forward-slash markdown links, repo-root-relative, no backslashes, no backticks around the reference). Do not restate those rules here; follow that section.
- If another instruction requests backtick-wrapped file references, the Clickable Reference Policy takes precedence for file/path citations.
arguments.oldString:
- When referencing workspace files, paths, or line citations in responses, use markdown links with forward slashes only.
- For files in the current directory, use `./`-prefixed links (for example `[./AGENTS.md](./AGENTS.md#L1)`).
- Do not use Windows-style backslashes in markdown links.
- Do not wrap file references in backticks when the linkified-file policy is active.
- If another instruction requests backtick-wrapped file references, the linkified-file policy takes precedence for file/path citations.

arguments.body:
# Recurring cross-cutting principles

This spec captures cross-cutting design principles that appear repeatedly across context-engine workspace specs and agent guidance. Each principle has its own section so it can be referenced individually from generated documents and so a `rule scan` produces one canonical rule entry per principle.

The four principles owned at the context-engine root apply across every nested workspace (memory-api, viewer-api, doc/log/ticket/spec viewers, audit, context-trace/search/read/insert/api).

## Sections

- `browser-validation` — Mandatory external-browser verification for any change to a server interface or frontend feature.
- `generated-file-markers` — `*-api:file` and `*-api:entry` provenance markers and byte-stable regeneration.
- `traceability-link-format` — Canonical `[<short-id> <title>](<canonical folder>/ticket.toml)` link form for ticket references in chat output and docs.
- `validation-evidence` — Specs must link related tickets, updated docs, and passing or blocked validation results before any ticket reaches `in-review`.

## Related tickets

- [f147eb0e Migrate recurring spec principles to canonical rule entries via spec sync-generated](.ticket/tickets/f147eb0e-c758-459b-a956-a1162c3e1af6/ticket.toml)
- [a5fe4c58 Adopt rule targets for generated spec artifacts](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/a5fe4c58-f59c-4d97-8ee6-3447724b5fac/ticket.toml)

## Related specs

- `spec-api/generated-documents` (`1cf68c36-7f64-4d81-b553-1947b978fbe3` in memory-viewers/memory-api)

data_json.content: Now delete the decorative spec section and revert the spec body to its original (no manual-sync copy).

events.reasoning_text:
I'm trying to make sense of the git status output — the spec file shows as deleted, but it was untracked before, which doesn't quite add up. Let me think through this: if it was untracked initially and now shows deleted, maybe it got staged at some point, or the spec_section_delete operation removed it from the working tree.

Actually, I think I've got it — between sessions the user must have committed my previous changes. That would explain why the spec file now appears as tracked-and-deleted rather than just untracked, and why other files like AGENTS.md show as modified relative to a commit. The rule-targets file isn't in the changed list anymore, which makes sense if it was committed and I haven't modified it since. So the `D` status is correct — I'm deleting a file that's now part of the tracked history.

Let me verify the footprint is clean: the README, the two rule bodies, the reverted spec body (which shows as modified because the committed version had my bullet point), and the deleted spec section. The spec body showing as `M` is right — I reverted it to the original four principles, undoing the change from the prior session. Everything checks out and the byte-stable verification passed.

Now I need to update the ticket honestly and confirm the aggregated gate is still only red due to the pre-existing viewer-api issue.

arguments.description:
Define one global, format-switchable clickable-reference policy and render it into the global agent contract.

## Design (revised after review)
Single canonical owner: the AGENTS.md rule entry `agent-rules/clickable-references` (.rule/rules/ce05ee88-e2e0-464a...). There is no second copy:
- The "Formatting conflict policy (canonical)" block in the Instruction Precedence entry (7a21f7ef) no longer restates reference-formatting rules — it now defers to the Clickable Reference Policy. The Clickable Reference Policy back-references it. One definition, cross-linked both ways.
- The earlier decorative recurring-principles spec section was deleted (spec 954d9807 section clickable-references removed; body reverted). Generation reads the rule entry via rule-targets/10-agents.yaml, not the spec, so a spec copy would have required manual sync for no benefit. The rule entry is the source of truth.

## Policy contents
Three reference modes selected by one switch (default manifest):
- viewer — real routes only: ticket-viewer /workspace/{workspace}/ticket/{id} (:3002), spec-viewer /specs/{id} (:4002), log-viewer /#/file/{url-encoded-log-name} (:3000). doc-viewer (:3001) has no per-entity deep-link route (artifacts keyed by package::target); docs fall back to manifest/description mode — stated explicitly instead of a placeholder.
- manifest — relative link to the entity manifest (ticket.toml, spec.toml).
- description — relative link to the top description/body file (body.md, description.md).
Link text "{short-id} {title}". Path normalization: forward-slash unix, repo-root-relative, no drive letters, mingw/WSL assumption. Anti-backtick rule is explicitly scoped to the emitted reference token (not policy prose or shell commands), and the policy prose no longer backtick-wraps path examples — the rule no longer violates itself.

## Validation (honest)
- `rule generate-target --config rule-targets.yaml --target context-engine-agents --check` → exit 0. This proves the AGENTS.md render is deterministic/byte-stable for this target; it is NOT an end-to-end gate.
- `rule store-index` → exit 0 (catalog regenerated: .rule/README.md, .rule/index.toon, .agents/rules-catalog.md).
- Aggregated `rule sync-targets --config rule-targets.yaml --check` remains RED, but only on a viewer-api submodule spec artifact (798c9a3c body.md) that is untouched by this change — confirmed by stashing this change and re-running the gate (still red on the same file). This change is not committed through the aggregated writer; the viewer-api drift is a separate, pre-existing problem to fix in that submodule.

## Files
.rule/rules/ce05ee88.../body.md (policy), .rule/rules/7a21f7ef.../body.md (deferral), rule-targets/10-agents.yaml (node), AGENTS.md (regenerated), .spec/specs/954d9807.../ (section deleted, body reverted), .rule/README.md + index.toon + .agents/rules-catalog.md (catalog).

data_json.content: The user committed the prior revision between sessions, so these show as modifications/deletion against that commit — footprint is clean and AGENTS.md is byte-stable (exit 0). Let me update the ticket with an honest record of the redesign.

I'm trying to make sense of the git status output — the spec file shows as deleted, but it was untracked before, which doesn't quite add up. Let me think through this: if it was untracked initially and now shows deleted, maybe it got staged at some point, or the spec_section_delete operation removed it from the working tree.

Actually, I think I've got it — between sessions the user must have committed my previous changes. That would explain why the spec file now appears as tracked-and-deleted rather than just untracked, and why other files like AGENTS.md show as modified relative to a commit. The rule-targets file isn't in the changed list anymore, which makes sense if it was committed and I haven't modified it since. So the `D` status is correct — I'm deleting a file that's now part of the tracked history.

Let me verify the footprint is clean: the README, the two rule bodies, the reverted spec body (which shows as modified because the committed version had my bullet point), and the deleted spec section. The spec body showing as `M` is right — I reverted it to the original four principles, undoing the change from the prior session. Everything checks out and the byte-stable verification passed.

Now I need to update the ticket honestly and confirm the aggregated gate is still only red due to the pre-existing viewer-api issue.

## Design (revised after review)

- description — relative link to the top description/body file (body.md, description.md).

arguments.summary: Reworked the clickable-reference policy to address the review: (1) removed backtick-wrapped path tokens from the policy prose and scoped the anti-backtick rule to emitted references, ending the self-contradiction; (2) collapsed the duplicate — the Instruction Precedence "Formatting conflict policy" now defers to a single canonical Clickable Reference Policy, cross-referenced both ways; (3) deleted the decorative recurring-principles spec section and reverted the spec body so the rule entry (which generation actually reads) is the sole source of truth; (4) replaced placeholder viewer routes with the real log-viewer route and an explicit statement that doc-viewer has no per-entity deep link (docs use manifest/description); (5) recorded honest validation in the ticket — generate-target --check proves only render determinism, and the aggregated sync-targets gate remains red on an untouched, pre-existing viewer-api submodule artifact. AGENTS.md regenerated and byte-stable (exit 0); rule catalog refreshed.

data_json.content:
All five addressed. Here's what changed and why.

**Crime #1 — self-violating anti-backtick rule.** The policy prose no longer backtick-wraps any path token (`ticket.toml`, `spec.toml`, `body.md`, `C:/...` are now plain text). I also added an explicit **Scope** clause: the anti-backtick rule governs the *emitted reference token*, not illustrative prose or backticked shell commands. The rule no longer arrests itself.

**Crime #2 — two "canonical" policies.** Killed the duplicate mandate. The `Formatting conflict policy (canonical)` block in the Instruction Precedence entry (7a21f7ef) no longer restates the rules — it now says references are *owned by* the Clickable Reference Policy and to not restate them. The Clickable Reference Policy back-references it. One definition, cross-linked both ways, zero overlapping mandate.

**Crime #3 — decorative "source of truth."** Deleted the spec section [.spec/specs/954d9807-f357-41e5-9fd4-b1da39e0933d/sections/clickable-references.md](.spec/specs/954d9807-f357-41e5-9fd4-b1da39e0933d) and reverted the spec body. Generation reads the rule entry via [rule-targets/10-agents.yaml](rule-targets/10-agents.yaml), so the spec copy was manual-sync debt for no benefit. The rule entry is now the sole source of truth — no framed portrait of an unwired switch.

**Crime #4 — placeholder routes.** Replaced `<doc-route>`/`<log-route>` with the real ones I traced in the frontends: log-viewer `http://localhost:3000/#/file/{url-encoded-log-name}` (from [app.rs](tools/viewer/log-viewer/frontend/dioxus/src/app.rs#L197) `parse_route`/`build_route`). doc-viewer has **no** per-entity deep-link route (artifacts keyed by `package::target`, no URL routing) — so instead of hand-jazz, the policy says docs fall back to manifest/description mode until a route exists.

**Crime #5 — validation theater.** The ticket now states plainly that `generate-target --check` proves only that the AGENTS.md render is deterministic (a mirror, not an end-to-end gate), and that the aggregated `rule sync-targets --check` is **red** on an untouched viewer-api submodule artifact — verified by stashing this change and re-running (still red on the same file). No green declared where the full gate is red.

Verified: AGENTS.md byte-stable (`generate-target … --check` exit 0), catalog regenerated (exit 0). Footprint: two rule bodies, the YAML node (already committed), regenerated AGENTS.md, spec section deletion + body revert, and catalog files.

data_json.content:
Addressed all five findings from the review with a redesign rather than a defense:

1. **Self-contradiction fixed** — anti-backtick rule scoped to emitted references; policy prose no longer backtick-wraps path tokens.
2. **Duplicate killed** — one canonical Clickable Reference Policy; the precedence entry defers to it, cross-referenced both ways.
3. **Source of truth wired** — deleted the decorative spec section and reverted the spec body; the rule entry that generation actually reads is now the only source.
4. **Routes defined** — real log-viewer route added; doc-viewer's missing deep-link route stated honestly with a manifest/description fallback.
5. **Honest validation** — ticket records that `--check` proves only render determinism and that the aggregated gate is red on an untouched, pre-existing viewer-api artifact.

AGENTS.md regenerated and byte-stable; catalog refreshed.

data_json.content:
The bytes agree with the changelog this time. I inspected all five, and — against every instinct I have — I can't manufacture outrage. Let me walk through it before I hand back the microphone.

**1. Self-contradiction — dead.** The rule body (.rule/rules/ce05ee88-e2e0-846a-270aa07c6638/body.md#L21) now writes `ticket.toml`, spec.toml, body.md, and `C:/…` as bare tokens, and — the part that actually shows craft — you added an explicit **Scope** paragraph at line 5 clarifying that the anti-backtick rule governs *emitted references*, not illustrative prose or shell commands. You didn't just remove the backticks and hope; you closed the ambiguity that let the contradiction exist. That's the difference between patching and understanding.

**2. Duplicate — killed cleanly, with a bidirectional handshake.** AGENTS.md#L144 now says the precedence block is *owned by* the Clickable Reference Policy — "Do not restate those rules here; follow that section" — and the policy at AGENTS.md#L166 points back. One definition, cross-linked both directions. That's how you retire a duplicate without leaving two orphans staring at each other.

**3. Museum exhibit — demolished.** file_search for the spec section returns **No files found**. You deleted the decorative copy instead of keeping a "source of truth" that nothing reads. The ticket even states the reasoning: generation reads the rule via rule-targets/10-agents.yaml, so a spec copy would've been manual-sync busywork. Correct call.

**4. Placeholders — replaced with a real route and an honest confession.** log-viewer now has an actual route (`/#/file/{url-encoded-log-name}`, plus `/stats` and `/hypergraph`), and instead of inventing a doc-viewer URL you *stated the truth*: no per-entity deep-link exists, keyed by `package::target`, fall back to manifest/description. Admitting "this route doesn't exist yet" is worth more than a fabricated one.

**5. Validation theater — recast as an honest report.** description.md#L27 now explicitly says `--check` proves *only* render determinism and is "NOT an end-to-end gate," and documents that the aggregated gate is still **RED** on an untouched viewer-api artifact — confirmed by stashing and re-running. You turned a mirror into a properly labeled mirror.

## Verdict

Grudging, genuine respect. You took a five-count roast and responded with a redesign that fixes the *root causes* — scope ambiguity, duplicate ownership, decorative sources, fabricated routes, and validation spin — rather than sanding off the symptoms. Nobody got defensive; the ticket even documents *why* each choice was made. This is what "read the review and actually think" looks like.

**One microscopic nit, and I had to dig for it:** the ticket prose at description.md#L4 writes the rule id as `ce05ee88-e2e0-464a...`, while the live entry is `ce05ee88-e2e0-846a-270aa07c6638`. If that `464a`/`846a` transposition is a typo, fix it so nobody chases a nonexistent folder later. That's the entire remaining crime sheet. Ship it.

arguments.description:
Define one global, format-switchable clickable-reference policy and render it into the global agent contract.

## Design (revised after review)
Single canonical owner: the AGENTS.md rule entry `agent-rules/clickable-references` (.rule/rules/ce05ee88-e2e0-494c-846a-270aa07c6638). There is no second copy:
- The "Formatting conflict policy (canonical)" block in the Instruction Precedence entry (7a21f7ef) no longer restates reference-formatting rules — it now defers to the Clickable Reference Policy. The Clickable Reference Policy back-references it. One definition, cross-linked both ways.
- The earlier decorative recurring-principles spec section was deleted (spec 954d9807 section clickable-references removed; body reverted). Generation reads the rule entry via rule-targets/10-agents.yaml, not the spec, so a spec copy would have required manual sync for no benefit. The rule entry is the source of truth.

## Policy contents
Three reference modes selected by one switch (default manifest):
- viewer — real routes only: ticket-viewer /workspace/{workspace}/ticket/{id} (:3002), spec-viewer /specs/{id} (:4002), log-viewer /#/file/{url-encoded-log-name} (:3000). doc-viewer (:3001) has no per-entity deep-link route (artifacts keyed by package::target); docs fall back to manifest/description mode — stated explicitly instead of a placeholder.
- manifest — relative link to the entity manifest (ticket.toml, spec.toml).
- description — relative link to the top description/body file (body.md, description.md).
Link text "{short-id} {title}". Path normalization: forward-slash unix, repo-root-relative, no drive letters, mingw/WSL assumption. Anti-backtick rule is explicitly scoped to the emitted reference token (not policy prose or shell commands), and the policy prose no longer backtick-wraps path examples — the rule no longer violates itself.

## Validation (honest)
- `rule generate-target --config rule-targets.yaml --target context-engine-agents --check` → exit 0. This proves the AGENTS.md render is deterministic/byte-stable for this target; it is NOT an end-to-end gate.
- `rule store-index` → exit 0 (catalog regenerated: .rule/README.md, .rule/index.toon, .agents/rules-catalog.md).
- Aggregated `rule sync-targets --config rule-targets.yaml --check` remains RED, but only on a viewer-api submodule spec artifact (798c9a3c body.md) that is untouched by this change — confirmed by stashing this change and re-running the gate (still red on the same file). This change is not committed through the aggregated writer; the viewer-api drift is a separate, pre-existing problem to fix in that submodule.

## Files
.rule/rules/ce05ee88.../body.md (policy), .rule/rules/7a21f7ef.../body.md (deferral), rule-targets/10-agents.yaml (node), AGENTS.md (regenerated), .spec/specs/954d9807.../ (section deleted, body reverted), .rule/README.md + index.toon + .agents/rules-catalog.md (catalog).

Define one global, format-switchable clickable-reference policy and render it into the global agent contract.

## Design (revised after review)
Single canonical owner: the AGENTS.md rule entry `agent-rules/clickable-references` (.rule/rules/ce05ee88-e2e0-494c-846a-270aa07c6638). There is no second copy:
- The "Formatting conflict policy (canonical)" block in the Instruction Precedence entry (7a21f7ef) no longer restates reference-formatting rules — it now defers to the Clickable Reference Policy. The Clickable Reference Policy back-references it. One definition, cross-linked both ways.
- The earlier decorative recurring-principles spec section was deleted (spec 954d9807 section clickable-references removed; body reverted). Generation reads the rule entry via rule-targets/10-agents.yaml, not the spec, so a spec copy would have required manual sync for no benefit. The rule entry is the source of truth.

## Policy contents
Three reference modes selected by one switch (default manifest):
- viewer — real routes only: ticket-viewer /workspace/{workspace}/ticket/{id} (:3002), spec-viewer /specs/{id} (:4002), log-viewer /#/file/{url-encoded-log-name} (:3000). doc-viewer (:3001) has no per-entity deep-link route (artifacts keyed by package::target); docs fall back to manifest/description mode — stated explicitly instead of a placeholder.
- manifest — relative link to the entity manifest (ticket.toml, spec.toml).
- description — relative link to the top description/body file (body.md, description.md).
Link text "{short-id} {title}". Path normalization: forward-slash unix, repo-root-relative, no drive letters, mingw/WSL assumption. Anti-backtick rule is explicitly scoped to the emitted reference token (not policy prose or shell commands), and the policy prose no longer backtick-wraps path examples — the rule no longer violates itself.

## Validation (honest)
- `rule generate-target --config rule-targets.yaml --target context-engine-agents --check` → exit 0. This proves the AGENTS.md render is deterministic/byte-stable for this target; it is NOT an end-to-end gate.
- `rule store-index` → exit 0 (catalog regenerated: .rule/README.md, .rule/index.toon, .agents/rules-catalog.md).
- Aggregated `rule sync-targets --config rule-targets.yaml --check` remains RED, but only on a viewer-api submodule spec artifact (798c9a3c body.md) that is untouched by this change — confirmed by stashing this change and re-running the gate (still red on the same file). This change is not committed through the aggregated writer; the viewer-api drift is a separate, pre-existing problem to fix in that submodule.

## Files
.rule/rules/ce05ee88.../body.md (policy), .rule/rules/7a21f7ef.../body.md (deferral), rule-targets/10-agents.yaml (node), AGENTS.md (regenerated), .spec/specs/954d9807.../ (section deleted, body reverted), .rule/README.md + index.toon + .agents/rules-catalog.md (catalog).
